title: polypogon monspeliensis var. subspathaceus
intro: Polypogon monspeliensis var. subspathaceus, also known as dwarf hair grass, is a variety of the perennial grass species Polypogon monspeliensis, native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Polypogon monspeliensis var. subspathaceus include treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
